reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Review target spans 18 files and 14671 LOC across provider selection, daemon supervision, vector storage, cache, reindex, retention, startup, and shutdown surfaces.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Inventory pass selected cross-phase hotspots where startup provider choice, active metadata, model-server liveness, reindex jobs, cache keys, and shutdown durability meet.

findingDetails.title: Fresh daemon bootstrap can ignore an explicit embedding provider override
Cancelling a running embedder reindex does not stop the worker before completion
Direct hf-model-server startup can unlink a live Unix socket without the launcher guard

searchLedger.hypothesis: Fresh daemon bootstrap may persist an auto-selected local embedder before explicit provider config is honored.

searchActions.result: ensureActiveEmbedder runs and active shard attaches before resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig.
ensureActiveEmbedder delegates to autoSelectActiveEmbedder and persists metadata.
auto-select probes local providers before cloud providers.
factory provider resolution gives explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER precedence.

searchLedger.rationale: The startup bootstrap and runtime provider-resolution contracts can select different providers on a fresh DB.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A running reindex job may ignore cancellation and still complete the model switch.

searchActions.result: runJob loops on the initial total and completes without rereading job status.
cancelJob marks non-terminal jobs cancelled, including running jobs.
Existing test coverage only cancels a queued job before it runs.

searchLedger.rationale: The control plane can report cancellation while the worker continues to completion and flips active metadata.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The direct model-server binary may reclaim a live Unix socket without the supervised liveness guard.

searchActions.result: Direct listener unlinks target on EADDRINUSE before retrying.
Supervised listener refuses reclaim when a live resident pid owns the socket.
Live test covers SIGKILL stale-socket residue, not direct live-resident collision.

searchLedger.rationale: The direct binary has weaker reclaim semantics than the launcher path, leaving an operational footgun.

searchLedger.invariant: Embedding cache keys should separate content by profile, input kind, model, and dimension.

searchActions.result: Primary key includes content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, and dimensions.
Scoped lookup requires profile_key and input_kind when options are supplied.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Cache profile/input-kind scoping is present in schema and lookup path.
searchLedger.rationale: No cache key collision finding in this pass.

searchLedger.invariant: Shutdown should drain watcher work before closing/checkpointing SQLite WAL.

searchActions.result: Shutdown drains fileWatcher before vectorIndex.closeDb().
close_db checkpoints active vector shard WAL and main WAL before closing.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Drain-before-close ordering and checkpoint calls are present in the reviewed code.
searchLedger.rationale: No shutdown WAL finding in this pass.

graphEvents.label: P1 TCP model-server target lacks loopback/auth perimeter

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Security review target spans embedding transport, model-server supervision, launcher env forwarding, IPC bridge, and memory-save locking across the hardening program.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected security-critical surfaces for socket/TCP target resolution, model-server request exposure, launcher env propagation, IPC connection options, and standalone-save locking.

findingDetails.title: TCP model-server targets can expose unauthenticated embed traffic outside loopback
Workflow locks fail open after timeout, re-admitting concurrent standalone writers

searchLedger.hypothesis: TCP/http model-server targets may bypass the Unix-socket perimeter and expose unauthenticated embed traffic outside loopback.

searchActions.result: tcp/http/https targets normalize to tcp host:port and server.listen binds the supplied hostname.
POST /api/embed accepts input strings and returns embeddings without auth/token checks.
client accepts the same tcp/http target forms and posts raw embedding inputs to /api/embed.
launcher forwards HF_EMBED_SERVER_URL and SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR into children.
No loopback-only or auth guard found; tests only assert localhost tcp acceptance.

searchLedger.rationale: The hardened Unix socket perimeter is bypassed by unchecked TCP targets that carry sensitive embedding inputs.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Filesystem lock timeout may fail open and let a second standalone save enter the write/index path.

searchActions.result: cross-process filesystem lock returns false on non-EEXIST errors and after 30s timeout.
withWorkflowRunLock executes the operation even when fsLockAcquired is false.
per-folder PFD lock also times out and then executes the operation unlocked.
packet requires preserving the single-writer invariant for standalone saves.

searchLedger.rationale: The lock code warns but still runs the protected write/index operation, so a slow first save can overlap with a second process.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Direct Unix socket binding may have a symlink/ownership race separate from the prior direct EADDRINUSE unlink finding.

searchActions.result: supervised path has symlink and foreign-owner guards for Unix socket dirs/nodes.
direct server mkdirs/chmods but does not call the supervision ownership guard.

searchLedger.deferredReason: Potentially same direct-binary perimeter family as prior DR-001-P2-001; defer to avoid duplicate reporting until traceability can confirm intended direct-binary contract.
searchLedger.rationale: Evidence suggests weaker direct-server Unix path handling, but prior finding already covers direct binary socket reclaim.

searchLedger.invariant: Provider diagnostics should not log raw API keys or secrets.

searchActions.result: No raw secret logging found in reviewed embedding/server paths; provider placeholder warnings name env vars but not values.
embedder_status returns provider info and model-server errors, but no API key material is assembled in this handler.

searchLedger.rationale: No raw credential emission was found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Reviewed diagnostics and exact secret searches did not expose raw API key/token values.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Traceability review spans nine phase/spec child packets plus committed implementation and test evidence across embedding stack and daemon reliability surfaces.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the phase docs named by the traceability dimension, then cross-checked gated/deferred claims against committed code and ENV/test evidence.

findingDetails.title: Daemon child packets leave required verification gates open while claiming implementation status
Phase 005 file-change matrix still points idle eviction at the wrong implementation file

searchLedger.hypothesis: Implemented daemon child packets may leave verification and strict-validation gates unreconciled across tasks/checklist/implementation-summary.

searchActions.result: Found repeated unchecked verification rows and pending strict validation claims in implemented packets.
Summary claims build, focused vitest, empirical daemon check, and strict validation passed while task ledger remains open.

searchLedger.rationale: The release gate cannot rely on conflicting completion ledgers.

searchLedger.invariant: Flag-flip and dtype decisions should remain gated/default-off unless live model evidence exists, and docs should say so.

searchActions.result: Summary documents onnxruntime blocker, default-off flag, q8 dtype, and cache re-deferral.
Code keeps skill-advisor model server opt-in and dtype q8; ENV reference documents gate.

searchLedger.rationale: The high-risk gated/deferred items are honestly documented and match code state.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Default-off flag, unchanged dtype, and live-model blocker are explicitly documented and reflected in code.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Phase 005 spec file matrix may still describe a pre-design implementation location after code moved idle eviction into supervision.

searchActions.result: Spec assigns idle eviction to hf-model-server.cjs.
Summary and code put idle eviction in model-server-supervision.cjs.

searchLedger.rationale: Localized spec-code matrix drift remains even though the summary explains the final design.

searchLedger.invariant: Phase 004 deferrals to phase 005 should either be closed or explicitly re-deferred by phase 005.

searchActions.result: Phase 004 marks live measurements, batch sweep, and cache-into-reindex as deferred to 005.
Phase 005 explicitly re-defers cache-into-reindex and keeps live numbers gated on the onnxruntime blocker.

searchLedger.rationale: No silent drop found for the phase 004 deferred work; phase 005 documents the follow-up gate.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Deferred work is carried forward explicitly with blocker rationale.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Maintainability target spans embedding provider code, model-server launcher/supervision paths, reindex/cache behavior, tests, and ENV reference documentation across the hardening program.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected maintainability-sensitive surfaces where new embedding behavior exposes operator knobs, test-only configuration, and documented support contracts.

findingDetails.title: Embedding env knobs are code/test-visible but absent from ENV_REFERENCE

searchLedger.hypothesis: New embedding env knobs may be code-visible while missing from the canonical operator reference.

searchActions.result: Found code/test hits for all four names and no ENV_REFERENCE.md hits.
Embedding table documents HF server URL/readiness/loading/cache knobs but omits prefix and reindex batch-size knobs.
Tests actively mutate EMBEDDER_REINDEX_BATCH_SIZE and HF_EMBEDDINGS_PREFIX_DOC, proving these are supported behaviors rather than dead comments.

searchLedger.rationale: The operator reference is the maintenance contract for env knobs; undocumented code/test-visible knobs drift from that contract.

searchLedger.invariant: Prefix override behavior should be directly covered for at least one kind and share the same resolver branch for both kinds.

searchActions.result: Both document and query prefix env keys are selected through the same getPrefixFor branch.
Document prefix override is covered; query override has no separate assertion but shares the resolver branch.

searchLedger.rationale: Missing query-specific assertion is not enough for a separate finding because the implementation uses one branch for both env keys.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Document override test covers the shared resolver path; doc drift is already captured in DR-004-P2-001.

searchLedger.invariant: Maintainability pass should distinguish real dead code from compatibility scaffolding.

searchActions.result: Found compatibility and legacy comments but no new blocking dead-code marker in the reviewed scope.

searchLedger.rationale: The actionable hidden-alias/doc drift is represented by DR-004-P2-001; the remaining legacy markers are compatibility comments, not standalone maintainability defects.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No additional dead-code marker had enough impact or novelty for a separate finding.

graphEvents.label: P1 live respawn lock can be reclaimed by age

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Cross-phase correctness revisit spans embedding startup/reindex, daemon shutdown/WAL, and launcher/model-server single-writer lease surfaces.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected cross-phase integration seams called out by iteration state: lazy model-server lock ownership, launcher shutdown, daemon WAL close ordering, active embedder startup, reindex worker lifecycle, and standalone save locks.

findingDetails.title: Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them
findingDetails.claim: The demand listener holds the respawn lock for the whole listener lifetime, but stale detection reclaims locks older than 60 seconds even when the owner PID is alive.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked release sites and cross-launcher tests; immediate contention is covered, but no test covers a live-owner lock older than RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The age threshold is valid for orphan cleanup of short critical sections, but it conflicts with the documented long-lived lazy listener lock.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another guard prevents a second launcher from reaching acquireDemandRespawnLock after 60 seconds while the first listener remains alive.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A lock intentionally held for the lazy listener lifetime can be age-reclaimed while the owner process is alive.

searchActions.result: RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS is 60000; isRespawnLockStale reclaims age-old locks even after confirming only that dead PIDs are stale; comments say the lock is intentionally held across the listener window.
Immediate second launcher blocking is covered; live-owner aged-lock blocking is not.

searchLedger.rationale: The stale policy contradicts the long-lived lock ownership contract and can reopen duplicate listener races.

searchLedger.invariant: Shutdown should drain watcher work before checkpointing and closing the vector DB/WAL.

searchActions.result: fileWatcher.close is awaited before vectorIndex.closeDb.
close_db checkpoints attached vector and main WAL before close and removes the unclean marker only on main checkpoint success.

searchLedger.rationale: No new shutdown/WAL ordering finding in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Drain-before-close and checkpoint-before-close ordering are present in the reviewed code.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Prior correctness findings may be rediscovered in the embed startup/reindex seam.

searchActions.result: Observed the same prior provider-order and cancellation lifecycle risks; no new extension beyond existing registry entries.

searchLedger.rationale: Already captured by DR-001-P1-001 and DR-001-P1-002; this iteration reports new findings only.
searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Prior findings were confirmed but not extended or escalated.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The standalone save lock and daemon lease check may contain a new cross-phase correctness issue beyond the prior workflow fail-open finding.

searchActions.result: Observed the prior fail-open timeout path and shared daemon liveness probe; no distinct new integration issue found.

searchLedger.rationale: No new standalone-save finding beyond DR-002-P1-002.
searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Existing registry already covers the actionable fail-open behavior.

focus: correctness/security single-writer-lease lifecycle deepening + final saturation sweep

graphEvents.label: P1 demand spawn failure strands lazy listener

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Final stabilization pass spans model-server demand listener ownership, respawn lock lifetime, launcher cross-process election, workflow locks, and embedding transport/security surfaces.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the single-writer and model-server lease lifecycle surfaces highlighted by iteration state, then swept adjacent launcher, bridge, provider, workflow-lock, and tests for duplicate election/security classes.

findingDetails.title: Demand-triggered model-server spawn failures strand the lazy listener
findingDetails.claim: handleModelServerDemand removes the demand listener before it knows the real model server launched. If launch returns false because the child has no PID, later clients have no lazy listener even though the response reports loading; if launch throws synchronously, releaseDemandRespawnLock is skipped.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked model-server unit and cross-launcher tests for failed spawn/no-PID demand coverage and for a finally-protected lock release path; found success-path and contention coverage but no failed-launch listener re-arm assertion.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Spawn failure may be rare with process.execPath and many child_process failures are emitted asynchronously, but launch explicitly returns false for a no-PID child and the demand handler currently treats that as a loading response after removing the listener.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if supported runtimes guarantee spawnFn cannot return a no-PID child or throw synchronously, or if another production path immediately re-arms the demand listener after launched:false.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A demand-triggered launch failure can remove the lazy listener without re-arming it and can skip lock release on synchronous launch errors.

searchActions.result: Demand path clears/unlinks the listener before launch, keeps the respawn lock, then releases the lock only after launch returns.
launch can return false when the spawned child has no valid pid.
Tests cover successful lazy launch, cross-launcher lock contention, and booting-window backoff, but no no-pid/failed demand launch recovery.

searchLedger.rationale: A failed launch should fail closed or re-arm the listener; the current ordering can leave neither a listener nor a model-server child.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The live-owner respawn lock may still be age-reclaimed after 60 seconds.

searchActions.result: Confirmed the same live-owner age-reclaim behavior from iteration 5.
Already registered as DR-005-P1-001; not re-reported.

searchLedger.rationale: The issue remains active but is already captured by a prior finding.

searchLedger.invariant: A sibling launcher should not bind or spawn while another live listener or live booting model server owns the socket.

searchActions.result: Prepare path checks live socket health, holds the demand respawn lock, respects a live recorded pid during spawn-to-bind, and releases the lock on listen failure.
Immediate second launcher and booting-window cases are covered.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cross-launcher election race beyond DR-005-P1-001 and DR-006-P1-001 was found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and existing tests cover the immediate contention and booting-pid election branches.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Final sweep may uncover a new transport/auth issue beyond the prior TCP unauthenticated exposure.

searchActions.result: Observed the same TCP/http target and unauthenticated embed request surfaces already captured in DR-002-P1-001; no distinct new auth path found.
Bridge tcp socket conversion is the same configured target channel; no additional credential-bearing path found.

searchLedger.rationale: The actionable transport issue remains DR-002-P1-001; this sweep found no new transport/security finding.
searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Prior finding already covers the unauthenticated TCP exposure class.

searchLedger.invariant: Embedding selection/status paths should not log or return raw provider secrets.

searchActions.result: Bearer headers are constructed for outbound probes, but the reviewed status/error paths do not print raw key values.

searchLedger.rationale: No new raw secret leakage was found in the final sweep.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Exact search found secret-bearing outbound headers but no raw-value logging or status return path.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Adjudication covered ten accumulated findings across provider bootstrap, reindex cancellation, TCP transport, workflow locks, daemon traceability ledgers, model-server locks/listeners, socket reclaim, file-matrix drift, and env reference drift.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected only the files cited by the ten accumulated findings plus directly relevant counter-evidence locations from prior deltas; no new discovery sweep was performed.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-001-P1-001 evidence still supports explicit provider override being bypassed during fresh bootstrap.

searchActions.result: ensureActiveEmbedder persists an auto-selected provider before startup provider config resolution; factory still gives explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER precedence.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-001-P1-002 evidence still supports running cancellation being ignored by the worker.

searchActions.result: cancelJob marks non-terminal jobs cancelled; runJob loops and completes without rereading status.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-002-P1-001 evidence still supports unauthenticated non-loopback TCP exposure.

searchActions.result: TCP/http targets remain accepted without loopback/auth guards; /api/embed accepts POSTed input strings without token checks.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-002-P1-002 evidence still supports fail-open workflow locks.

searchActions.result: Both global and per-folder lock acquisition can return false, and callers still run the operation regardless.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-003-P1-001 evidence still supports conflicting daemon child completion ledgers.

searchActions.result: Open verification rows remain in tasks/checklists while implementation summaries/spec status claim implemented and some checks PASS.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds as release traceability debt; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-005-P1-001 evidence still supports live-owner respawn lock age expiry.

searchActions.result: The lock stale predicate still age-expires locks after 60000ms while comments say the demand lock is intentionally held for the listener lifetime.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-006-P1-001 evidence still supports demand-spawn failure stranding the lazy listener.

searchActions.result: Demand handler releases listener before launch; launch can return false on no PID; lock release is not in a finally-protected block.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-001-P2-001 evidence still supports weaker direct socket reclaim semantics.

searchActions.result: Direct binary still unlinks Unix socket on EADDRINUSE; supervised listener refuses to reclaim when live resident PID is recorded.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; final severity remains advisory P2.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-003-P2-001 evidence still supports phase 005 file-matrix drift.

searchActions.queryOrPath: phase 005 spec matrix and implementation summary/code
searchActions.result: Spec still assigns idle eviction to hf-model-server.cjs; summary and code identify model-server-supervision.cjs.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; final severity remains advisory P2.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-004-P2-001 evidence still supports env reference drift.

searchActions.result: Prefix overrides, reindex batch size, and HF_LOCAL_MODEL are code-visible; the embedding env table still omits them.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; final severity remains advisory P2.

graphEvents.label: P1 non-active tracked DBs skip TRUNCATE checkpoint

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Correctness/data-safety sweep spans cache migration and eviction, SQLite WAL durability across canonical/shard schemas, daemon shutdown ordering, retention maintenance, and embed batching behavior.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the under-explored focus areas named in the iteration prompt, plus directly relevant tests for checkpoint order, byte chunking, and retention maintenance.

findingDetails.title: Non-active tracked SQLite connections skip the TRUNCATE checkpoint on shutdown

searchLedger.invariant: Cache eviction should enforce global, per-profile, and query byte budgets while preserving shard-qualified table access.

searchActions.result: cacheTable switches to active_vec when attached; migration, lookup recency, store, and LRU byte-budget deletion all use cacheTable(db).
Tests cover fresh schema, legacy backfill, scoped lookup, global/profile/query byte caps, shrink_memory, and legacy lookup compatibility.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cache eviction or shard-table migration defect found in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Reviewed implementation and targeted tests cover the named byte-budget/LRU and shard-qualified table behavior.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Shutdown may apply the TRUNCATE checkpoint sequence only to the active singleton, leaving prior tracked custom DB connections to detach/close without the explicit durability guarantee.

searchActions.result: initialize_db(custom_path) creates tracked connections; close_db loops all tracked conns but only checkpoints the active db after non-active conns are closed.
Existing tests assert active DB and active shard checkpointing, and separately exercise switching active DBs, but do not assert checkpointing for every tracked connection on shutdown.

searchLedger.rationale: The same close path that explicitly rejects passive close as insufficient for the active DB relies on passive close for every non-active tracked DB.

searchLedger.invariant: Fatal shutdown should drain file-watcher work before closing the vector DB so drains cannot reopen DB after the TRUNCATE checkpoint.

searchActions.result: fatalShutdown awaits fileWatcher.close before vectorIndex.closeDb and documents the drain-before-close invariant.
file-watcher close path tracks in-flight tasks and rejects queued work during close, matching the shutdown drain design.
Test asserts vectorIndex.closeDb appears after the fileWatcher drain in fatalShutdown.

searchLedger.rationale: The daemon-level ordering invariant is present and covered; the new WAL finding is confined to vector-index-store's handling of multiple tracked DB connections.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: fatalShutdown drains before closeDb and has direct source-order coverage.

searchLedger.invariant: Retention deletes should run FTS optimize and guarded incremental vacuum/checkpoint maintenance after the delete transaction.

searchActions.result: post-delete maintenance runs after sweepTx, optimizes FTS, checks auto_vacuum before incremental_vacuum, and truncates WAL best-effort.
Tests assert optimize/auto_vacuum/wal_checkpoint run outside the transaction and are skipped when no rows are deleted.

searchLedger.rationale: No new retention sweep correctness defect found.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The requested post-delete FTS optimize and incremental_vacuum guards are implemented and tested.

searchLedger.invariant: Embedding batches should chunk by count and byte budget, preserve output order/null slots, retry only bounded transient readiness failures, and adopt custom dimensions consistently.

searchActions.result: Router chunks while respecting both SPECKIT_EMBED_CLIENT_MAX_BATCH and MAX_EMBED_REQUEST_BYTES before calling provider.embedBatch.
HF local embedPrepared uses two attempts for retryable readiness errors, validates row count and dimensions, and embedBatch preserves null slots for skipped inputs.
Tests cover byte-budget splitting, batched POST row counts, null-slot preservation, truncated response rejection, and one-time dimension adoption for custom models.

searchLedger.rationale: No new batching/concurrency defect found in the reviewed client/server embedding path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Implementation and tests cover the requested count+byte chunking, bounded retry, and dimension adoption behavior.

graphEvents.label: P1 reindex shard writer skips WAL TRUNCATE checkpoint

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Final sweep spans the whole embedding-stack hardening scope and revisits WAL lifecycle, provider selection, dimension adoption, batching, cache, and vector persistence paths.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected last-chance hotspots named in the iteration prompt plus direct consumers that prove whether provider fallback or dimension drift can persist wrong-sized vectors.

findingDetails.title: Reindex shard writer bypasses the WAL TRUNCATE durability path
findingDetails.claim: writeVectorsToShard opens a direct WAL-backed shard connection, writes vector rows, and closes without the explicit TRUNCATE checkpoint required elsewhere for at-rest durability.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked for connection tracking, checkpointAllWal, per-shard wal_checkpoint before close, and post-reindex attach checkpointing; none are present.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: SQLite WAL is recoverable with the WAL file, but the hardening program explicitly treats passive close as insufficient for at-rest durability.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the durability contract is narrowed to daemon shutdown only or passive close is accepted for reindex shard writers.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A reindex-created shard connection can write WAL-backed vector data and close without the explicit TRUNCATE checkpoint required by the vector-index close path.

searchActions.result: writeVectorsToShard opens a new Database, enables WAL, writes vectors, and closes the shard without wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE).
The active pointer flips after the shard write and attachActiveVectorShard attaches the shard, but no checkpoint occurs in that transition.
Checkpoint tests cover vector-index-store active close and retention, not the reindex shard writer.

searchLedger.rationale: This is distinct from DR-008-P1-001 because the shard connection is never registered in db_connections and therefore cannot be fixed only by checkpointing all tracked connections.

searchLedger.invariant: HF local must adopt server/custom dimensions once, reject row-count or row-dimension mismatches, normalize vectors, and preserve batch ordering/null slots.

searchActions.result: adoptEmbeddingDimension reads server dimension, validates first row and every mapped row, l2-normalizes returned vectors, and embedBatch maps prepared outputs back to original indexes.

searchLedger.rationale: No new dimension-adoption or normalization finding.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Implementation validates response shape and dimensions before returning vectors.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Partial provider availability could silently switch to a different-dimension provider and persist incompatible vectors.

searchActions.result: Bootstrap auto-select remains local-first: ollama, hf-local, openai, voyage.
Factory fallback records dimensionChanged metadata and returns the fallback provider; it warns but does not itself persist vectors.

searchLedger.rationale: Provider fallback sharp edge exists, but persistence/search paths separately validate embedding length before vector writes.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No evidence that fallback dimension drift bypasses vector write validation in this scope.

searchLedger.invariant: Wrong-sized embeddings must not be marked success or inserted into active vec tables.

searchActions.result: index and update paths reject embeddings whose length differs from get_embedding_dim before vector insertion/success marking.
Cache lookup is dimension-keyed and generated cache writes store actual embedding length; execution router resolves dimensions from override/profile.

searchLedger.rationale: No new data drop/duplication path from wrong-dimensional embeddings in the reviewed persistence path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Dimension checks fire before vector insert/success state changes.

reason: 9 discovery + 1 adjudication; ratio plateaued ~0.10 then 0.05 post-adjudication; all 4 dims saturated; 7 sub-areas ruled clean; verdict CONDITIONAL 0P0/9P1/3P2
